title: Lesson Quiz: Digital vs Physical Portfolios
description: Assessment of the concept that Tools and formats.

questions.prompt: What is one advantage of a digital portfolio?

choices.text: It cannot include links or images
choices.feedback: Digital portfolios can often include links, images, and other media.

choices.text: It removes the need to organize content
choices.feedback: Digital portfolios still need strong organization and clear categories.

choices.text: It is always the best format in every situation
choices.feedback: The best format depends on the audience, purpose, and situation.

choices.text: It can be easier to update and share
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital portfolios are often easier to revise and share with others.

questions.prompt: Which is a possible advantage of a physical portfolio?

choices.text: It cannot be reviewed in person
choices.feedback: A physical portfolio can be useful during in-person review.

choices.text: It can provide printed materials that are easy to handle directly
choices.feedback: Correct. Physical portfolios can be useful when a reviewer wants to see printed samples in person.
Physical portfolios still need a clear structure.
Multimedia features are more commonly associated with digital formats.

questions.prompt: The best portfolio format depends on the student's purpose and audience.

choices.feedback: Correct. Students should choose the format that fits the situation, purpose, and audience.
Format choice should be guided by purpose and audience, not by a one-size-fits-all rule.

questions.prompt: A student wants to email a portfolio link quickly to several reviewers. Which format is likely the better choice?

choices.text: A large binder that cannot be copied
choices.feedback: A physical binder may be harder to share quickly with multiple people.

choices.text: A digital portfolio that can be shared online
choices.feedback: This is a strong choice, but the correct answer in this set is the more specific statement about quick sharing with multiple reviewers.

choices.text: A digital portfolio that can be updated and sent by link
choices.feedback: Correct. A digital format is usually better for quick sharing and updates.

choices.text: A stack of loose papers with no labels
choices.feedback: Loose papers are hard to share and review effectively.

questions.prompt: Both digital and physical portfolios can be effective when they are organized well and used for the right purpose.

choices.feedback: Correct. Either format can work well if it matches the purpose and is organized clearly.
Both digital and physical formats can be effective depending on the situation.
